channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Nigerian man extradited to US for alleged sextortion scheme that led to SC teen’s suicide
item.description: A Nigerian man accused of running a sextortion scheme that led to a South Carolina teenager killing himself, has been extradited to the U.S. after being indicted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Executives at Japan`s Fuji TV and parent firm resign over a sex scandal linked to a former star
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — The president and chairperson of Japanese network Fuji Television have resigned over a widening sex assault scandal linked to one of Japan's top TV celebrities.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump pushes border crackdown at House Republican retreat as he caps first week in office
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump regaled House Republicans on Monday with a rhetorical highlight reel from his first week in office, and he urged them to support his immigration crackdown and border security proposals. “We have no apologies and we’re moving very fast," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, WILL WEISSERT and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: DOJ fires prosecutors who worked on Trump criminal cases
item.description: Justice Department officials fired several prosecutors who worked on President Trump's criminal cases, saying they could not “trust” them.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: Woman gets more than 3-year prison term for helping pregnant Chinese women get to US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California woman was sentenced Monday to more than 3 years in prison in a long-running case over a business that helped pregnant Chinese women travel to the United States to deliver babies who automatically became American citizens. U.S. District Judge R.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Full-scale replica of Anne Frank's hidden annex opens in NYC
item.description: A full-scale replica of the secret annex where Anne Frank penned her famous diary has opened in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo And Joseph Frederick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: California utility reports fault on power line miles away from origin of deadly Eaton fire
item.description: GLENDALE, Calif. (AP) — Southern California Edison on Monday reported a fault on a power line connected miles away from ones located near the origin of the Eaton Fire, the deadly blaze that ignited outside of Los Angeles on Jan. 7 and killed at least 17 people.
item.dc:creator: JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Escaped Alabama inmate captured more than 600 miles away at Buc-ee's in Texas
item.description: Law enforcement officials confirm an Alabama inmate was captured at a Buc-ee's in Texas after traveling more than 600 miles away from the jail he escaped from.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prosecutors say woman charged in killing of Border Patrol agent was in contact with homicide suspect
item.description: A Washington state woman charged in connection with the fatal shooting of a U.S. border patrol agent in Vermont had been in frequent contact with someone whom authorities have linked to homicides in Pennsylvania and California, a federal prosecutor said in court documents Monday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately
item.description: U.S. public health officials have been told to stop working with the World Health Organization, effective immediately.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE AP Medical Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nigerian extradited to US to face charges over nude photo that led to teen's death
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A Nigerian man has been extradited to the U.S. and charged with causing the death of a South Carolina teen who took his own life after the suspect posed as a woman and tried to extort the teen after he sent nude photos, prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Costco shareholders double down on DEI practices
item.description: Costco shareholders resoundingly voted down a proposal that urged management to look into the business risk of DEI initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Super Bowl footballs are ready to go within hours of the matchup being set
item.description: As soon as this year's Super Bowl matchup was set, workers at the Wilson Sporting Goods football factory jumped into action. The factory in the rural village of Ada, Ohio, makes the game balls used by every NFL team along with many of the nation’s top college programs and high schools.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A fact sheet for Monday’s executive order refers to transgender Americans as “trans-identifying,” a technical term used by some to delegitimize transgender identities.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Trump's first executive actions on climate and environment
item.description: President Donald Trump's first week in office included a flurry of executive orders with implications for Earth's climate and environment.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Starbucks offering free refills to all customers, bringing back condiment bar
item.description: Starbucks is implementing new and old policies at its cafes in the United States and Canada to improve the customer experience. Beginning on Monday, customers who order a “for here” beverage can also get free refills of hot or iced coffee or hot or iced tea during their visit.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: DeepSeek hit by 'large-scale' cyberattack Monday
item.description: (KRON) -- Chinese AI startup DeepSeek announced Monday that it had been hit by a large-scale cyberattack. The one-year-old startup launched its R1 AI model last week and has since drawn comparisons to OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Protesters take to the Texas Capitol after ICE operations in Austin
item.description: On Sunday afternoon, a crowd of protestors gathered at the Texas Capitol after U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement raids were conducted in Austin.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Torres, NewsNation

item.title: Kia recalls more than 80,000 U.S. autos due to improper air bag deployment and improper wiring
item.description: Automaker Kia America is recalling more than 80,000 vehicles due to floor wiring beneath the front passenger seat that can become damaged and prevent airbags and seat belts from deploying properly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Southern California rain helps firefighters but creates risk of flooding and toxic ash runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rain fell on parts of Southern California on Sunday and the scattered showers were expected to continue overnight, boosting the risk of toxic ash runoff in areas scorched by Los Angeles-area wildfires. Flood watches were in effect through 4 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bills cornerback Christian Benford ruled out of AFC title game vs. Chiefs with a concussion
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Buffalo Bills cornerback Christian Benford was ruled out of the AFC title game against the Kansas City Chiefs on Sunday night with a concussion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: court records
item.description: A settlement has been reached in the lawsuit involving an Arkansas-based couple and stars of HGTV's "Fixer to Fabulous" just days before the parties were set to go to trial.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: Nuns have snowball fight during winter storm
item.description: Several nuns duked it out last week, having a snowball fight during a winter storm that swept across the Deep South.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mississippi state lawmaker introduces 'Contraception Begins at Erection Act' with fines for masturbation
item.description: A Democratic Mississippi state senator introduced legislation this week that makes it unlawful for men to masturbate "without the intent to fertilize an embryo," with the lawmaker criticizing anti-abortion measures that only "focus on the woman’s role.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
